title: R package dev / enhancement protocol
text: New args appended, defaulted (usually NULL). Existing calls unchanged. Behavior-changing default = justify (correct + compatible). Note NEWS. Package-wide switch → options() + default set .onLoad (R/zzz.R), “set unset” idiom.

title: R package dev / enhancement protocol
text: Data → helper-*.R fixtures, inline. Every bug fixed → regression test fails pre-fix. Global state (options/env/wd) → withr::local_* auto-restores. Expensive/optional → skip--default (env-var guard + skip_if_not_installed()). Snapshots: commit real _snaps/ changes; revert LF↔︎CRLF-churn.

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: Drive operations go adapter. Create one gdpins_real_drive() supplying project root folder ID (visible folder’s Drive URL): Optional pre-flight auth call (usually unnecessary construct real adapter right away):
code: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/<your-folder-id-here> # Picks up GDRIVE_EMAIL by default: adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms")  # Or a path string (resolved automatically via googledrive::drive_get): adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("My Drive/kazLandEconImpact-data")  # Optional explicit override: adapter <- gdpins_real_drive(   "1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms",   email = "you@example.com" ) gdpins_ensure_drive_auth()

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: wiring boards, confirm right folder: can also pass Drive folder ID directly drive_path know exactly folder use. gdpins verifies folder exists errors — though default check happens board first used, ; see Lazy connection :
code: # Returns a clickable browser URL for any folder in the adapter root gdpins_drive_url(adapter)                              # root folder gdpins_drive_url(adapter, "kazLandEconImpact-data/data-raw")  # subfolder bd_raw <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_raw",   drive_path = "1AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOpQrStUvWxYz0123456",  # folder ID directly   cache_dir  = file.path(Sys.getenv("OD_PRIV_ROOT"), "data-raw-cache"),   adapter    = adapter )

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: board wraps layer data pipeline. Three configurations:
code: # Local only — no Drive, works fully offline (no adapter needed) bd_local <- gdpins_init_board(   name      = "scratch",   local_dir = file.path(Sys.getenv("OD_PRIV_ROOT"), "scratch") ) # Drive + cache — standard production configuration bd_raw <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_raw",   drive_path = "kazLandEconImpact-data/data-raw",   cache_dir  = file.path(Sys.getenv("OD_PRIV_ROOT"),                          "kazLandEconImpact-data", "data-raw-cache"),   adapter    = adapter,   versioned  = TRUE,   create     = NA,          # prompt to create if absent   on_discrepancy = NULL     # "prompt" interactively / "warn" in scripts ) # Drive + cache + standalone local (super config) bd_clean <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_clean",   drive_path = "kazLandEconImpact-data/data-clean",   cache_dir  = file.path(Sys.getenv("OD_PRIV_ROOT"),                          "kazLandEconImpact-data", "data-clean-cache"),   local_dir  = file.path(Sys.getenv("OD_PRIV_ROOT"),                          "kazLandEconImpact-data", "data-clean-local"),   adapter    = adapter,   versioned  = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: three chunks cost nothing. gdpins_init_board() Drive work: records asked connects first time actually use board. script sets bd_local, bd_raw bd_clean ever reads bd_raw pays one board, three. Connecting costs — online probe, Drive existence/create check, resolving folder ID, building pins boards, on_discrepancy sync check. triggered first verb touches board contents: gdpins_pin_read(), gdpins_pin_write(), gdpins_board_status(), gdpins_sync(), friends. Printing board connect . catch init-time errors move first use: mistyped drive_path, missing folder create = FALSE, create = NA prompt, sync warning surface later used . rather find immediately, connect purpose: opt entirely, per board globally: See ?"lazy-boards" full picture.
code: gdpins_board_is_connected(bd_raw)   # FALSE — nothing has touched it yet  gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "parcels")  # connects, then reads gdpins_board_is_connected(bd_raw)   # TRUE # Force the Drive check and sync check now gdpins_board_connect(bd_raw)  # ... or just for this connection, with a different discrepancy policy gdpins_board_connect(bd_clean, on_discrepancy = "sync_from_drive") bd_raw <- gdpins_init_board(name = "data_raw", drive_path = "...",                             cache_dir = "...", adapter = adapter,                             lazy = FALSE)  options(gdpins.lazy_boards = FALSE)   # every board, this session

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: Pin names bare snake_case — board encodes layer.
code: library(dplyr)  # Any R object — tibble, sf, list, nested tibble gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, my_tibble, "gdp_panel") gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, my_sf,     "parcels")    # sf auto-encoded as parquet gdp_panel <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "gdp_panel") parcels   <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "parcels")   # sf decoded with CRS intact # Remove a pin across all configured board layers (idempotent if missing) gdpins_pin_remove(bd_raw, "gdp_panel")

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: gdpins auto-detects storage format via gdpins_detect_format(). rules, order: Override format explicitly need : Rule thumb: let auto-detection choose. Override "rds" object list-columns non-spatial content (e.g. nested model output) want read back R without re-building.
code: gdpins_detect_format(mtcars)         # "parquet"  — plain data frame gdpins_detect_format(my_sf)          # "parquet"  — sf, sfc cols are fine gdpins_detect_format(my_model)       # "rds"    — not a data frame gdpins_detect_format(nested_tibble)  # "rds"    — has non-sfc list-column # Force RDS — useful for tibbles with custom attributes you want to preserve gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, my_tibble, "gdp_panel", format = "rds")  # Force Parquet — rarely needed; auto-detect handles most cases gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, my_tibble, "gdp_panel", format = "parquet")

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: sf objects stored Parquet geometry converted WKT text. CRS (EPSG code) embedded column name using suffix __<epsg>__: happens automatically — manual encoding/decoding needed: helpers gdpins_sf_to_parquet() gdpins_parquet_to_sf() exposed need encode/decode manually (e.g. write external Parquet file): Limitation: geometry columns must EPSG integer CRS. Custom proj-string-CRS error. Set CRS first sf::st_set_crs().
code: geometry column "geometry" with EPSG 4326   → stored as column "geometry__4326__" (WKT text)   → restored as column "geometry" (sfc) with CRS 4326 on read # Write sf — geometry and CRS encoded automatically gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, parcels_sf, "parcels")  # Read back — sf class and CRS fully restored parcels <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "parcels") sf::st_crs(parcels)$epsg  # original EPSG  # Multiple geometry columns with different CRS are each encoded separately gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, multi_geom_sf, "parcels_multi") encoded <- gdpins_sf_to_parquet(parcels_sf)   # tibble with geometry__4326__ col restored <- gdpins_parquet_to_sf(encoded)      # sf with CRS restored

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: Sometimes want geometry back raw WKT text — inspect , hand another tool, skip cost building sf object. Pass wkt_engine = "none" gdpins_pin_read(): geometry columns come back character vectors, names still carrying __<epsg>__ suffix. gdpins_as_sf() converts data frame back sf. autodetects geometry column (character column named like geom__4326__, geom, wkt) infers CRS name — trusting standard __<epsg>__ pattern silently, using non-standard digit run (geom_1111) message, falling back default_epsg warning name digits. Pass epsg (column, several candidates match) explicit.
code: txt <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "parcels", wkt_engine = "none") class(txt)                # a plain tibble; geometry is WKT text  parcels_again <- gdpins_as_sf(txt)           # autodetect column + EPSG sf::st_crs(parcels_again)$epsg  # Firmly set the CRS when the column name does not encode it df <- data.frame(geom = "POINT (0 0)") gdpins_as_sf(df, epsg = 4326)

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: geometry ↔︎ WKT conversion done one two interchangeable engines: "wk" (default) — wk package. 20× faster write sf, full-precision. "sf" — sf::st_as_text(), pinned digits = 15 also full-precision. dependency-light fallback. Select per call wkt_engine (gdpins_pin_write() / gdpins_pin_read() / gdpins_raw_put_object() / gdpins_raw_get()) engine (two helpers), set default whole session: engines read-compatible: WKT written one reads back correctly , switching engines never requires re-encoding stored data. Precision note: bare sf::st_as_text() uses getOption("digits") (7 significant figures), silently rounds projected coordinates (e.g. UTM metres) ~0.5 m. gdpins avoids engines — "wk" engine exact construction, "sf" engine called digits = 15.
code: # Per-call override gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, parcels_sf, "parcels", wkt_engine = "sf")  # Session-wide default options(gdpins.wkt_engine = "sf")  encoded_sf <- gdpins_sf_to_parquet(parcels_sf, engine = "sf")

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: versioned = TRUE (default), gdpins_pin_write() call creates new version rather overwriting. Versions labelled timestamp. versioned = FALSE board, write overwrites previous value. Use frequently-updated reference data history unwanted.
code: # Inspect all versions of a pin info <- gdpins_pin_info(bd_raw, "gdp_panel") info$versions        # tibble: version label, created, size info$n_versions      # integer count # Read the latest version (default) gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "gdp_panel")  # Read a specific historical version by its label gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "gdp_panel", version = "20240615T120000Z-abc12") # Keep only the 3 most recent versions, dry-run first gdpins_prune_pin_versions(bd_raw, "gdp_panel", keep = 3, dry_run = TRUE) gdpins_prune_pin_versions(bd_raw, "gdp_panel", keep = 3, dry_run = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: Drive unreachable (internet, VPN ), board falls back automatically connects: warning emitted; writes blocked (Drive boards write-protected offline); reads continue local copy. fallback automatic happens Drive unreachable init time. deliberately detach already-connected board raw connection Drive — e.g. avoid network calls , keep working flaky connection — use gdpins_go_offline() gdpins_go_online() instead. See “Deliberately going offline back online” vignette("google-drive-adapter") details.
code: # Check connectivity explicitly before Drive operations gdpins_is_online()   # TRUE / FALSE  # Board status shows sync state between Drive and local gdpins_board_status(bd_raw)  # Reads work offline — served from cache or local_dir gdp_panel <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "gdp_panel")  # no network needed

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: data received APIs — plain files, pins metadata.
code: conn <- gdpins_raw_connect(   drive_path = "kazLandEconImpact-data/raw-exogenous/worldbank-api",   local_path = file.path(Sys.getenv("OD_PRIV_ROOT"),                          "kazLandEconImpact-data", "raw-exogenous",                          "worldbank-api"),   adapter    = adapter,   create     = TRUE ) # Store an R object (serialised to the appropriate format by extension) gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, api_response_tbl, "gdp_2024.parquet")  # Upload a file verbatim (byte-faithful, any extension) gdpins_raw_put_file(conn, path = "downloads/kadaster_raw.geojson",                     name = "kadaster_raw.geojson")  # Retrieve (reads from local mirror; Drive only if absent locally) gdp_raw  <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet") kad_raw  <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "kadaster_raw.geojson")  # List contents — 8-column tibble including local paths and Drive IDs listing <- gdpins_raw_ls(conn, depth = 2) # listing$local_path  — absolute path on this machine # listing$drive_id    — Drive file/folder ID (NA on fake adapter) # listing$drive_url   — clickable browser URL (NA on fake adapter)  # Resolve a file to its local path, downloading from Drive if needed local_path <- gdpins_raw_path(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet") arrow::read_parquet(local_path)  # pass to any reader  # Non-standard filenames (spaces, parentheses) work too gdpins_raw_path(conn, "quarterly report (Q1 2024).csv")  # Remove one file (flat or nested path). Missing files are ignored. gdpins_raw_remove(conn, "kadaster_raw.geojson")

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: Sync always explicit — never automatic. Conflict handling versioned boards: sides simply become new versions (zero data loss). raw/unversioned boards: on_conflict = "stop" halts reports; "prompt" asks per conflict.
code: # Auto: newer side wins, both directions gdpins_sync(bd_raw)  # Explicit direction gdpins_sync(bd_raw, direction = "from_drive")   # pull Drive → local gdpins_sync(bd_raw, direction = "to_drive")     # push local → Drive  # Check status first gdpins_board_status(bd_raw)

title: Getting Started with gdpins
code: # List all pins gdpins_list_pins(bd_clean)  # Detailed info for one pin gdpins_pin_info(bd_clean, "parcels")

title: Getting Started with gdpins
code: library(ggplot2)  # Save a figure (PNG or SVG — ggplot object is NOT stored) gdpins_save_figure(   plot   = ggplot(gdp_panel, aes(year, value)) + geom_line(),   name   = "gdp_trend",   dir    = here::here("output", "figures"),   width  = 8,   height = 5,   device = "png" )  # Write output table to output board gdpins_pin_write(bd_output, summary_tbl, "regional_summary")  # Publish local output to Drive (deliberate final step) gdpins_publish_output(   tables_board  = bd_output,   figures_dir   = here::here("output", "figures"),   drive_tables  = "output-tables",   drive_figures = "output-figures",   adapter       = adapter,   dry_run       = TRUE   # preview first )  # Then actually publish: gdpins_publish_output(   tables_board  = bd_output,   figures_dir   = here::here("output", "figures"),   adapter       = adapter,   dry_run       = FALSE )

code: # Preview what would be removed (dry_run = TRUE by default) gdpins_prune_pin_versions(bd_raw, "gdp_panel", keep = 3, dry_run = TRUE)  # Actually prune gdpins_prune_board_versions(bd_raw, keep = 2, dry_run = FALSE, force = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with gdpins
text: Pin names bare snake_case. board encodes layer. concept keeps name (parcels, gdp_panel) flows raw → interm → clean, giving readable lineage without renaming. See docs/adr/0001-gdpins-data-architecture.md full architectural decision record. See also Google Drive Adapter vignette deeper guide adapter setup, folder ID lookup, gdpins_drive_url().

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: gdpins package isolates Google Drive operations behind Drive adapter (gdpins_drive_adapter). Every function touches Drive — boards, raw connections, sync, publish — receives adapter rather calling googledrive directly. seam lets swap real adapter fake (tempdir-backed) one tests without network calls.

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: Use gdpins_real_drive() create real adapter. must supply Drive folder ID project root folder — path string look .

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: Open folder Google Drive browser. URL looks like: last segment folder ID.
code: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
code: # Using a Drive folder ID (recommended): adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms")  # Using a path string (resolved via googledrive::drive_get): adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("My Drive/projects/myproject")

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: gdpins_real_drive() now calls gdpins_ensure_drive_auth() automatically. Set ~/.Renviron: explicit pre-flight gdpins_ensure_drive_auth() call still valid, optional creating adapters gdpins_real_drive().
code: # 1) Uses GDRIVE_EMAIL from .Renviron (recommended) adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("folder-id")  # 2) Explicit override adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("folder-id", email = "explicit@example.com")  # 3) No email set -> gargle interactive CLI account picker adapter <- gdpins_real_drive("folder-id", email = "") GDRIVE_EMAIL=you@example.com

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: Use gdpins_drive_url() get clickable URL folder within adapter root: Copy-paste URL browser visually confirm location connecting boards.
code: # Root folder URL: gdpins_drive_url(adapter)  # Subfolder URL: gdpins_drive_url(adapter, "data-raw")

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: can also supply Drive folder ID directly drive_path — useful know exact folder ID: drive_path folder ID, gdpins verifies exists errors immediately found. create argument ignored — folder IDs auto-created.
code: board <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_raw",   drive_path = "my-project/data-raw",   # relative to adapter root   cache_dir  = "~/.cache/gdpins/data-raw",   adapter    = adapter,   create     = TRUE ) board board <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_raw",   drive_path = "1AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOpQrStUvWxYz0123456",   cache_dir  = "~/.cache/gdpins/data-raw",   adapter    = adapter )

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: Nested uploads via gdpins_raw_put_object() gdpins_raw_put_file() create missing intermediate Drive folders automatically (example "sub/sub/folder/file.rds").
code: conn <- gdpins_raw_connect(   drive_path = "worldbank-api",   local_path = "data/raw/worldbank-api",   adapter    = adapter,   create     = TRUE ) conn

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: gdpins supports explicit, idempotent remove verbs: Drive removals recoverable: files moved Drive trash (hard-deleted). raw connections, removal scope single files ; recursive folder deletion intentionally unsupported.
code: # Boards: removes a pin from all configured board layers gdpins_pin_remove(board, "cars")  # Raw connections: removes one file (flat or nested path) gdpins_raw_remove(conn, "sub/sub/file.rds")

title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: scripts packages, use gdpins_fake_drive() offline testing: fake adapter mirrors Drive operations local filesystem — credentials, network, fully reproducible. Note gdpins_fake_drive() test seam, offline-mode substitute: starts empty tempdir, swapping gdpins_real_drive() board connection already working preserve existing Drive-side layout, typically surfaces create-confirm errors. temporarily disconnect real board connection Drive, see “Deliberately going offline back online” instead.
code: adapter <- gdpins_fake_drive()  board <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "test_board",   drive_path = "project/data-raw",   cache_dir  = tempfile("cache_"),   adapter    = adapter,   create     = TRUE ) board #> <gdpins_board> [DC-] v+ cfg=drive_cache name=test_board path=project/data-raw #> config: "drive_cache" #> name: "test_board" #> versioned: "TRUE" #> connected: "FALSE" #> drive: "project/data-raw" #> cache: "/tmp/RtmpqiDLsK/cache_24cf1e59cb3d"

what: Deliberately going offline and back online
title: Setting up the Google Drive Adapter
text: gdpins_init_board() gdpins_raw_connect() fall back local-automatically Drive unreachable (see “Offline behaviour” section vignette("gdpins-usage")). Sometimes want effect purpose — example, Drive flaky, working plane, just don’t want network calls slowing every read/write . gdpins_go_offline() detaches Drive already-connected board raw connection, without touching files: hood, gdpins_go_offline() reuses whichever local storage board already — standalone local_dir/local_board "drive_cache_local" board, cache_dir/cache_board "drive_cache" board standalone local directory (mirroring automatic offline fallback). Nothing copied deleted; returned object just stops touching Drive. original Drive configuration travels returned object, gdpins_go_online() can restore later: gdpins_go_online() requires gdpins_is_online() TRUE errors x never produced gdpins_go_offline() — restores specific prior connection rather guessing one. on_discrepancy takes values gdpins_init_board()/gdpins_raw_connect() ("prompt", "warn", "sync_from_drive", "sync_to_drive", "ignore"), control exactly offline changes get reconciled Drive. Drive credentials expired offline, pass freshly authenticated adapter: gdpins_go_online(board_offline, adapter = gdpins_real_drive("folder-id")). two functions work raw connections (gdpins_raw_connect()) way — gdpins_go_offline(conn) / gdpins_go_online(conn).
code: adapter <- gdpins_fake_drive() board <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_raw",   drive_path = "project/data-raw",   cache_dir  = tempfile("cache_"),   local_dir  = tempfile("local_"),   adapter    = adapter,   create     = TRUE )  board_offline <- gdpins_go_offline(board) #> ℹ Board "data_raw" switched to local-only (offline) mode. #> ℹ Drive is untouched; call `gdpins_go_online()` to reconnect and sync. board_offline$config   # "local_only" #> [1] "local_only"  # Reads and writes stay local -- no network calls, no blocked writes gdpins_pin_write(board_offline, mtcars, "cars") #> Creating new version '20260716T190834Z-c0340' #> Writing to pin 'cars' gdpins_pin_read(board_offline, "cars") #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>      mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1  21       6  160    110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 #>  2  21       6  160    110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 #>  3  22.8     4  108     93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 #>  4  21.4     6  258    110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 #>  5  18.7     8  360    175  3.15  3.44  17.0     0     0     3     2 #>  6  18.1     6  225    105  2.76  3.46  20.2     1     0     3     1 #>  7  14.3     8  360    245  3.21  3.57  15.8     0     0     3     4 #>  8  24.4     4  147.    62  3.69  3.19  20       1     0     4     2 #>  9  22.8     4  141.    95  3.92  3.15  22.9     1     0     4     2 #> 10  19.2     6  168.   123  3.92  3.44  18.3     1     0     4     4 #> # ℹ 22 more rows # Later, once Drive is reachable again: board_online <- gdpins_go_online(   board_offline,   on_discrepancy = "sync_to_drive"   # push what changed while offline ) board_online$config   # back to "drive_cache_local"

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: two ways store files: serialise R object, upload file verbatim.

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: extension name controls serialisation format. Supported formats: Files written local mirror Drive one step.
code: library(dplyr)  # Tibble → Parquet (fast, columnar) gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, gdp_panel_tbl, "gdp_2024.parquet")  # Tibble → CSV (plain text) gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, metadata_tbl, "metadata.csv")  # Any R object (list, model, etc.) → RDS gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, my_model, "model.rds")  # sf object → GeoJSON gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, parcels_sf, "parcels.geojson")  # sf object → Parquet (geometry encoded as WKT, CRS preserved) gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, parcels_sf, "parcels.parquet")  # Nested paths are created automatically gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, api_response, "worldbank/gdp_by_country_2023.parquet")

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: Use already file disk want upload byte-faithful, without R round-trip (e.g. downloaded CSV, shapefile ZIP, JSON blob):
code: # Upload a file exactly as-is gdpins_raw_put_file(conn,   path = "downloads/kadaster_raw.geojson",   name = "kadaster_raw.geojson" )  # Non-standard name — no escaping needed gdpins_raw_put_file(conn,   path = "downloads/quarterly report (Q1 2024).csv",   name = "quarterly report (Q1 2024).csv" )  # Nested destination gdpins_raw_put_file(conn,   path = "exports/final/merged.parquet",   name = "exports/final/merged.parquet" )

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: gdpins_raw_get() reads local mirror deserialises using format matched file extension. Use force_refresh = TRUE pull fresh copy Drive first.
code: # Read from local mirror (default — no network needed if already synced) gdp_tbl   <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet")     # → tibble via arrow meta_tbl  <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "metadata.csv")          # → tibble via readr my_model  <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "model.rds")             # → R object via readRDS parcels   <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "parcels.geojson")       # → sf via sf::st_read  # sf stored as parquet: geometry and CRS are automatically restored parcels   <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "parcels.parquet")       # → sf  # Force a re-download from Drive before reading gdp_fresh <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet", force_refresh = TRUE)  # Non-standard filenames work identically q1_tbl <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "quarterly report (Q1 2024).csv")

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: Sometimes need file path rather parsed R object — pass specialised reader, external tool, format one four built-ones. Use gdpins_raw_path():
code: # Get path (downloads from Drive if not yet local) path <- gdpins_raw_path(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet")  # Use any reader directly arrow::read_parquet(path) data.table::fread(path) vroom::vroom(path)  # Non-standard formats — gdpins_raw_get() doesn't parse these, use the path path_xlsx <- gdpins_raw_path(conn, "source data (raw).xlsx") readxl::read_excel(path_xlsx)

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: remember exact path. gdpins_raw_path(), gdpins_raw_get() gdpins_pin_read() resolve name pass actually : rule gdpins auto-resolves match exact unique. Anything looser reported rather acted , can never quietly read different file meant. Two basenames clash error listing full paths, coin flip. gdpins_raw_remove() opts entirely: matches exact path . deletes local copy, one verb must never guess.
code: # The file lives at "api/worldbank/gdp_2024.parquet" -- the basename is enough, # as long as it is unique. gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet")  # Case does not have to match either. gdpins_raw_get(conn, "GDP_2024.parquet")  # A near-miss is never guessed at -- you get told, with candidates: gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.csv") #> Error: File not found in raw connection: 'gdp_2024.csv' #> i Did you mean: #> * api/worldbank/gdp_2024.parquet #> i List everything with `gdpins_raw_get(conn, "*")`.

title: Working with Raw Files: Writing, Reading, Listing, and Local Downloads
text: name containing * ? flips verbs listing mode — show matches instead acting . Nothing read; nothing deleted. quickest way answer “actually ?”, error messages point . Unlike gdpins_raw_ls() — whose depth = 2 default hides sub/sub/folder/notes.rds — listing mode always recurses whole tree. Matching case-sensitive every platform, "*.csv" match CARS.CSV. result ordinary tibble (just prints names), can go straight :
code: # Everything, at any depth gdpins_raw_path(conn, "*") #> 4 files #> * gdp_2024.parquet #> * metadata.csv #> * api/worldbank/gdp_2024.parquet #> * sub/sub/folder/notes.rds  # Every CSV, however deeply nested gdpins_raw_path(conn, "*.csv") big <- gdpins_raw_path(conn, "*.parquet") big[big$size > 1e6, c("name", "size")]

what: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: Google Drive + pins data management R projects. gdpins layers three storage models Google Drive reproducible, offline-capable data pipelines. Drive source truth; local cache mirrors . Reads local-first; writes fan Drive cache simultaneously; sync delete always explicit guarded.

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: Install GitHub: load -place project root:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("EBukin/gdpins") pkgload::load_all(".")

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: Set Google account email .Renviron: Auth lazy — triggered first Drive operation, call explicitly: Offline local-workflows need auth.
code: GDRIVE_EMAIL=you@example.com gdpins_ensure_drive_auth() gdpins_is_online()   # check connectivity

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: board wraps one layer data pipeline. Three configurations: None calls touch Drive. Boards lazy: records arguments connects first use, setting three boards reading one costs one connection, three. Printing board never connects . trade-: init-time errors sync warnings surface first use instead. Pass lazy = FALSE, set options(gdpins.lazy_boards = FALSE), get old timing back. See ?"lazy-boards". Pin names bare snake_case — board encodes pipeline layer.
code: # Local only — no Drive, works fully offline bd_local <- gdpins_init_board(   name      = "scratch",   local_dir = "path/to/local/folder" )  # Drive + cache — standard production configuration bd_raw <- gdpins_init_board(   name           = "data_raw",   drive_path     = "my-project-data/data-raw",   cache_dir      = "path/to/data-raw-cache",   versioned      = TRUE,   create         = NA,        # prompt to create if Drive folder absent   on_discrepancy = "prompt"   # "warn" for non-interactive scripts )  # Drive + cache + standalone local (super config) bd_clean <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_clean",   drive_path = "my-project-data/data-clean",   cache_dir  = "path/to/data-clean-cache",   local_dir  = "path/to/data-clean-local",   versioned  = TRUE ) gdpins_board_is_connected(bd_raw)   # FALSE — nothing has touched it yet gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "parcels")  # connects, then reads  # Rather find out now that the Drive path is wrong? Connect on purpose: gdpins_board_connect(bd_raw) # Write any R object — tibble, sf, list, nested tibble gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, gdp_tbl,  "gdp_panel") gdpins_pin_write(bd_raw, parcels,  "parcels")   # sf auto-encoded as parquet  # Read back gdp_panel <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "gdp_panel") parcels   <- gdpins_pin_read(bd_raw, "parcels")  # sf restored with CRS intact  # Need the file rather than the object? *_read returns objects, *_path returns paths path <- gdpins_pin_path(bd_raw, "parcels")

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: data received external sources — plain files, pins metadata.
code: conn <- gdpins_raw_connect(   drive_path = "my-project-data/raw-exogenous/worldbank-api",   local_path = "path/to/raw-exogenous/worldbank-api",   create     = TRUE )  # Store an R object (serialised by extension: .parquet, .csv, .rds, .geojson) gdpins_raw_put_object(conn, api_tbl, "gdp_2024.parquet")  # Upload a file verbatim (byte-faithful — no R round-trip) gdpins_raw_put_file(conn, path = "downloads/data.geojson", name = "data.geojson")  # Retrieve (reads local mirror; hits Drive only if absent locally) result <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet")  # Names resolve: a unique basename or the wrong case is enough. A near-miss is # reported with suggestions, never silently resolved to a different file. result <- gdpins_raw_get(conn, "gdp_2024.parquet")   # even if nested under api/  # "*" or "?" switches to listing mode -- shows what matches, reads nothing gdpins_raw_path(conn, "*")        # everything, at any depth gdpins_raw_path(conn, "*.csv")    # every CSV, however deeply nested  # List contents gdpins_raw_ls(conn, depth = 2)

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: Sync always explicit — never automatic. versioned boards, conflicts create new versions (zero data loss). raw unversioned boards, conflicts prompt interactively stop report. Nothing silently overwritten.
code: # Auto: newer side wins, both directions gdpins_sync(bd_raw)  # Explicit direction gdpins_sync(bd_raw, direction = "from_drive")   # pull Drive → local gdpins_sync(bd_raw, direction = "to_drive")     # push local → Drive  # Check drift first gdpins_board_status(bd_raw)   # in_sync / local_ahead / drive_ahead / offline

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
code: gdpins_list_pins(bd_clean)          # tibble: name, type, n_versions, size gdpins_pin_info(bd_clean, "parcels")  # Save a figure (PNG or SVG — ggplot object is not stored) gdpins_save_figure(plot = p, name = "gdp_trend", dir = "output/figures",                    width = 8, height = 5)  # Publish local output to Drive (always deliberate — dry_run = TRUE first) gdpins_publish_output(   tables_board  = bd_output,   figures_dir   = "output/figures",   drive_tables  = "output-tables",   drive_figures = "output-figures",   dry_run       = TRUE )

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: sf objects stored parquet geometry columns encoded WKT CRS (EPSG code) embedded column name (geom__4326__). round-trip lossless — CRS geometry restored automatically read. keep geometry raw WKT text read (sf restoration), pass wkt_engine = "none". gdpins_as_sf() converts data frame back sf, autodetecting geometry column inferring CRS name:
code: gdpins_sf_to_parquet(sf_obj)   # df with WKT geometry columns gdpins_parquet_to_sf(df)       # sf with original CRS restored txt <- gdpins_pin_read(board, "parcels", wkt_engine = "none")  # WKT text cols gdpins_as_sf(txt)                    # autodetect column + EPSG -> sf gdpins_as_sf(df, column = "geom", epsg = 4326)  # be explicit when unsure

title: gdpins – Google Drive + pins for R
text: geometry ↔︎ WKT conversion uses one two interchangeable engines, chosen wkt_engine / engine argument gdpins.wkt_engine option: "wk" (default) — uses wk package. ~20× faster write sf always full-precision. "sf" — uses sf::st_as_text() (pinned digits = 15, also full-precision). dependency-light fallback. read-compatible: WKT written one engine reads back correctly , can switch time without re-encoding stored data. Note: bare sf::st_as_text() defaults 7 significant digits, silently rounding projected coordinates (e.g. UTM metres) ~0.5 m. gdpins avoids engines. benchmark harness lives tests/testthat/test-benchmark-wkt.R (skipped unless GDPINS_BENCH_WKT=true).
code: gdpins_pin_write(board, sf_obj, "parcels")                    # default "wk" gdpins_pin_write(board, sf_obj, "parcels", wkt_engine = "sf") # force sf options(gdpins.wkt_engine = "sf")                             # switch globally

text: friendly, single-column decoder data whose geometry lives WKT character column. Unlike gdpins_parquet_to_sf() — strictly decodes every "<name>__<epsg>__" column reads CRS name — function autodetects geometry column infers CRS, also handles hand-made externally-produced columns (geom, geom_1111, ...) whose name carry clean EPSG code.

title: Convert a data frame with a WKT text column to an sf object (autodetecting) — gdpins_as_sf
text: x data frame WKT geometry column. column Character scalar NULL. Name WKT column. NULL autodetects (see Details). epsg Integer scalar NULL. EPSG code geometry CRS. NULL infers column's name (see Details). engine Character scalar, WKT engine: "wk" (default) "sf". NULL uses gdpins.wkt_engine option. See io-formats "WKT engine" section. default_epsg Integer scalar. CRS assumed epsg NULL column name carries digits. Default 4326 (WGS 84).

title: Convert a data frame with a WKT text column to an sf object (autodetecting) — gdpins_as_sf
text: sf object, — autodetection finds geometry column — input x unchanged (warning). standard __<epsg>__ suffix stripped converted column name; names kept -.

title: Convert a data frame with a WKT text column to an sf object (autodetecting) — gdpins_as_sf
text: Column detection. column NULL, character columns whose name matches standard __<epsg>__ suffix contains "geom"/"wkt" candidates. Exactly one candidate used automatically. none found, x returned unchanged warning (plain, non-spatial data passes safely). several match, error asks pass column. explicitly supplied column absent always error. CRS inference. epsg NULL, EPSG code taken column name: standard __<epsg>__ pattern trusted silently; non-standard digit run (e.g. geom_1111) used emits message; name digits falls back default_epsg warning. latter two cases firmly encouraged pass epsg explicitly. explicit epsg argument always wins silences inference. coordinate transformation performed; EPSG code labels CRS.

title: Convert a data frame with a WKT text column to an sf object (autodetecting) — gdpins_as_sf
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE pts <- st_sf(   id = 1:2,   geometry = st_sfc(st_point(c(71, 51)), st_point(c(76, 43)), crs = 4326) ) encoded <- gdpins_sf_to_parquet(pts)   # a "geometry__4326__" WKT column gdpins_as_sf(encoded)                  # autodetect column + EPSG, silent #> Simple feature collection with 2 features and 1 field #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 71 ymin: 43 xmax: 76 ymax: 51 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      id    geometry #>   <int> <POINT [°]> #> 1     1     (71 51) #> 2     2     (76 43)  # Non-standard name: EPSG inferred from the digits (with a message) df <- data.frame(geom_3857 = "POINT (0 0)") gdpins_as_sf(df) #> ℹ Inferred EPSG 3857 from non-standard column name "geom_3857". #> ℹ Pass `epsg` explicitly if this is not the intended CRS. #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 0 ymax: 0 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #>     geom_3857 #> 1 POINT (0 0)  # No CRS in the name: pass epsg explicitly to avoid the default + warning df2 <- data.frame(geom = "POINT (0 0)") gdpins_as_sf(df2, epsg = 4326) #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 0 ymax: 0 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>          geom #> 1 POINT (0 0)

title: Initialise a gdpins board — gdpins_init_board
text: name Character scalar. Board/layer label (e.g. "data_raw"). drive_path Character scalar. Drive path board (relative adapter root), NULL "local_only". cache_dir Character scalar. Local cache directory path, NULL. local_dir Character scalar. Standalone local board directory path, NULL. versioned Logical. Whether board stores pin versions. Default TRUE. create Logical NA. TRUE = create Drive board absent; FALSE = error absent; NA (default) = interactive CLI prompt error. on_discrepancy Character scalar NULL. One c("prompt","warn","sync_from_drive","sync_to_drive","ignore"). NULL resolves "prompt" interactively "warn" non-interactively. adapter gdpins_drive_adapter, NULL "local_only". lazy Logical NULL. TRUE (default) defers Drive work sync check board first used; FALSE call. NULL uses gdpins.lazy_boards option (default TRUE). See lazy-boards.

title: Initialise a gdpins board — gdpins_init_board

title: Initialise a gdpins board — gdpins_init_board
text: "local_only" — local_dir provided, drive_path/adapter. "drive_cache" — drive_path + adapter + cache_dir, local_dir. "drive_cache_local" — three: drive_path, cache_dir, local_dir. board checks sync discrepancies Drive local (governed on_discrepancy). Non-existent Drive boards never auto-created unless create = TRUE. default (lazy = TRUE) none happens init: board records arguments connects first use. Initialising several Drive boards free, pay ones touch. See lazy-boards forces connection error timing changes, gdpins_board_connect() force one purpose.

Reads engine-agnostic — WKT written either engine parses either — affects parsing speed. See io-formats "WKT engine" section.

title: Resolve a pin to its file path(s) on disk — gdpins_pin_path
text: Resolution mirrors gdpins_pin_read() exactly — local board, cache board, Drive — pin materialised (downloaded pins cache) Drive holds copy, just gdpins_raw_path() downloads demand.

title: Resolve a pin to its file path(s) on disk — gdpins_pin_path
text: gdpins_raw_path(), gdpins_raw_get() gdpins_pin_read() resolve name pass actually exists, stopping first hit: Exact relative path → resolve. Exact basename, unique → resolve silently ("cars.csv" finds "sub/cars.csv"). Exact basename, several matches → error listing every full path. Case-insensitive exact, unique → resolve silently. stem, different extension → error, suggesting ("cars.csv" "cars.parquet" exists). Close edit distance → error, suggesting 5 nearest. Nothing close → error naming connection, pointing listing mode. Auto-resolve happens rungs 1, 2 4, match exact unique. Rungs 3 5–7 ever suggest — never guess. Rung 4 also settles real platform difference: file.exists() case-insensitive Windows case-sensitive elsewhere, gdpins case-folding rather letting filesystem one platform . gdpins_pin_read() gdpins_pin_path() use ladder without rungs 2–3: pin names flat, "path" "basename" question. gdpins_raw_remove() uses rung 1 . hard-deletes local copy, never auto-resolves near-miss onto real file; missing target stays idempotent -op.

title: Resolve a pin to its file path(s) on disk — gdpins_pin_path
text: name containing * ? switches gdpins_raw_path(), gdpins_raw_get(), gdpins_raw_remove(), gdpins_pin_read() gdpins_pin_path() listing mode: return listing matches instead acting one item. Listing mode never bulk-reads never bulk-deletes. "*" — everything. "*.csv" — every .csv, depth (unlike gdpins_raw_ls(), whose depth = 2 default hides sub/sub/folder/file.rds). Matching case-sensitive every platform, "*.csv" match "CARS.CSV". Raw verbs return gdpins_raw_listing; pin verbs return gdpins_pin_listing. ordinary tibbles print method shows names .

title: Resolve a pin to its file path(s) on disk — gdpins_pin_path
text: governing rule across gdpins: *_get / *_read return objects, *_path returns paths. extension never switches mode. gdpins_raw_get() deserialises, accepts four formats gdpins knows read — .rds, .parquet, .geojson, .csv. Anything else error naming formats pointing gdpins_raw_path(). gdpins_raw_path() returns path extension, downloading Drive demand. escape hatch formats gdpins read: get path, hand whatever package . gdpins_raw_put_file() mirrors asymmetry. uploads bytes verbatim, accepts extension (.gpkg, .tif, .xlsx, …) insists one.

title: Read a file from a raw connection — gdpins_raw_get
text: gdpins_raw_path(), gdpins_raw_get() gdpins_pin_read() resolve name pass actually exists, stopping first hit: Exact relative path → resolve. Exact basename, unique → resolve silently ("cars.csv" finds "sub/cars.csv"). Exact basename, several matches → error listing every full path. Case-insensitive exact, unique → resolve silently. stem, different extension → error, suggesting ("cars.csv" "cars.parquet" exists). Close edit distance → error, suggesting 5 nearest. Nothing close → error naming connection, pointing listing mode. Auto-resolve happens rungs 1, 2 4, match exact unique. Rungs 3 5–7 ever suggest — never guess. Rung 4 also settles real platform difference: file.exists() case-insensitive Windows case-sensitive elsewhere, gdpins case-folding rather letting filesystem one platform . gdpins_pin_read() gdpins_pin_path() use ladder without rungs 2–3: pin names flat, "path" "basename" question. gdpins_raw_remove() uses rung 1 . hard-deletes local copy, never auto-resolves near-miss onto real file; missing target stays idempotent -op.

title: Read a file from a raw connection — gdpins_raw_get

Listing mode never bulk-reads never bulk-deletes. "*" — everything. "*.csv" — every .csv, depth (unlike gdpins_raw_ls(), whose depth = 2 default hides sub/sub/folder/file.rds). Matching case-sensitive every platform, "*.csv" match "CARS.CSV". Raw verbs return gdpins_raw_listing; pin verbs return gdpins_pin_listing. ordinary tibbles print method shows names .

title: Read a file from a raw connection — gdpins_raw_get

Anything else error naming formats pointing gdpins_raw_path(). gdpins_raw_path() returns path extension, downloading Drive demand. escape hatch formats gdpins read: get path, hand whatever package . gdpins_raw_put_file() mirrors asymmetry. uploads bytes verbatim, accepts extension (.gpkg, .tif, .xlsx, …) insists one.

title: Synchronise a board or raw connection with Drive — gdpins_sync
text: "Newer" decision (strongest signal per layer): Boards: compare pins version id/timestamp. Raw files: compare MD5 (drive's md5Checksum). mtime tiebreaker. Conflict handling: Versioned boards: writes simply become versions (pins handles , zero loss). on_conflict effectively "version" regardless. Raw / unversioned boards on_conflict = "stop": abort report conflicting items; change nothing. Raw / unversioned boards on_conflict = "prompt": ask user interactively per conflict. Never silent overwrite. Writes syncs blocked offline (gdpins_is_online() FALSE). informative error raised.

"sf" — uses sf::st_as_text() (digits = 15, full-precision) write sf::st_as_sfc() read. Kept fallback encoding never depends single engine. engines read-compatible: WKT written one reads back correctly . default can changed globally options(gdpins.wkt_engine = "sf"). Note: sf::st_as_text() defaults getOption("digits") (7 significant figures), silently rounds projected coordinates (e.g. UTM metres) ~0.5 m. gdpins always pins digits = 15 "sf" engine avoid ; "wk" engine full-precision construction.

text: gdpins_go_offline(x) strips Drive-facing components x returns "local_only" object backed whichever local storage x already disk: "drive_cache_local" boards keep using standalone local_board / local_dir — directory user already working . "drive_cache" boards (standalone local dir) fall back cache_board / cache_dir, mirroring automatic offline fallback gdpins_init_board(). Calling gdpins_go_offline() object already "local_only" -op. gdpins_go_online(x) reverses : requires x produced gdpins_go_offline() (.e. carry stashed state), checks gdpins_is_online(), reattaches stashed Drive adapter path (freshly supplied adapter), runs discrepancy check used gdpins_init_board() / gdpins_raw_connect() — governed on_discrepancy — anything written offline reconciled Drive.

title: Temporarily disconnect from Drive, then reconnect and sync later — offline-mode
code: adapter <- gdpins_fake_drive() board <- gdpins_init_board(   name       = "data_raw",   drive_path = "my-project/data-raw",   cache_dir  = tempfile("cache_"),   local_dir  = tempfile("local_"),   adapter    = adapter,   create     = TRUE )  # Work disconnected for a while -- writes/reads stay local board_offline <- gdpins_go_offline(board) #> ℹ Board "data_raw" switched to local-only (offline) mode. #> ℹ Drive is untouched; call `gdpins_go_online()` to reconnect and sync. board_offline$config   # "local_only" #> [1] "local_only" gdpins_pin_write(board_offline, mtcars, "cars") #> Creating new version '20260716T190826Z-c0340' #> Writing to pin 'cars'  # Reconnect and push local changes back up to Drive board_online <- gdpins_go_online(board_offline, on_discrepancy = "sync_to_drive") #> Syncing "data_raw" to Drive (on_discrepancy = "sync_to_drive"). #> ✔ Board "data_raw": synced "cars" local -> Drive. #> ✔ Board "data_raw" reconnected to Drive ("drive_cache_local"). board_online$config    # "drive_cache_local" #> [1] "drive_cache_local"

title: Raw-exogenous connection — raw-connection
text: governing rule across gdpins: *_get / *_read return objects, *_path returns paths. extension never switches mode. gdpins_raw_get() deserialises, accepts four formats gdpins knows read — .rds, .parquet, .geojson, .csv. Anything else error naming formats pointing gdpins_raw_path(). gdpins_raw_path() returns path extension, downloading Drive demand. escape hatch formats gdpins read: get path, hand whatever package . gdpins_raw_put_file() mirrors asymmetry. uploads bytes verbatim, accepts extension (.gpkg, .tif, .xlsx, …) insists one.

title: Raw-exogenous connection — raw-connection
text: gdpins_raw_path(), gdpins_raw_get() gdpins_pin_read() resolve name pass actually exists, stopping first hit: Exact relative path → resolve. Exact basename, unique → resolve silently ("cars.csv" finds "sub/cars.csv"). Exact basename, several matches → error listing every full path. Case-insensitive exact, unique → resolve silently. stem, different extension → error, suggesting ("cars.csv" "cars.parquet" exists). Close edit distance → error, suggesting 5 nearest. Nothing close → error naming connection, pointing listing mode. Auto-resolve happens rungs 1, 2 4, match exact unique. Rungs 3 5–7 ever suggest — never guess. Rung 4 also settles real platform difference: file.exists() case-insensitive Windows case-sensitive elsewhere, gdpins case-folding rather letting filesystem one platform . gdpins_pin_read() gdpins_pin_path() use ladder without rungs 2–3: pin names flat, "path" "basename" question. gdpins_raw_remove() uses rung 1 . hard-deletes local copy, never auto-resolves near-miss onto real file; missing target stays idempotent -op.

title: Raw-exogenous connection — raw-connection
text: name containing * ? switches gdpins_raw_path(), gdpins_raw_get(), gdpins_raw_remove(), gdpins_pin_read() gdpins_pin_path() listing mode: return listing matches instead acting one item. Listing mode never bulk-reads never bulk-deletes. "*" — everything. "*.csv" — every .csv, depth (unlike gdpins_raw_ls(), whose depth = 2 default hides sub/sub/folder/file.rds). Matching case-sensitive every platform, "*.csv" match "CARS.CSV". Raw verbs return gdpins_raw_listing; pin verbs return gdpins_pin_listing. ordinary tibbles print method shows names .
